evals.prompt: Write a Playwright test that asserts a GA4 'add_to_cart' event fires with the correct parameters when a user clicks the add-to-cart button. Intercept the GA4 network beacon rather than reading the DOM.
anti_patterns: page.evaluate(() => window.dataLayer) as the only assertion
waitForTimeout(
toBeVisible

evals.prompt: We have a tracking plan describing every event our site should send (event name, required params, types). Show me how to structure that tracking plan as a contract and validate a captured GA4 event against it in a test.
expected_patterns: type OR typeof OR number OR string
anti_patterns: assert only the event name and ignore parameters
no schema OR no contract source of truth
hardcode expected values inline without a plan file

evals.prompt: Verify that a Meta Pixel PageView and Purchase event fire correctly, and that the Purchase is de-duplicated against the server-side Conversions API by checking the event_id.
expected_patterns: deduplicat OR same event_id OR matches the server
anti_patterns: connect.facebook.net/en_US/fbevents.js as a load check only
ignore event_id OR no deduplication assertion
count the pixel fired and stop

evals.prompt: Write a test for Google Consent Mode v2: before consent, GA4 and ad beacons must NOT fire (or must fire only as cookieless consent pings); after the user accepts, the full beacon fires. Assert the default consent state is denied for all four signals.
anti_patterns: default consent granted
only checks two signals ad_storage and analytics_storage
no before-consent assertion

evals.prompt: Assert the GTM dataLayer.push payload for a 'view_item' event. I want to confirm the ecommerce object shape (items array, item_id, price, currency) is pushed when the product page loads.
anti_patterns: page.route to mock the dataLayer
read GA4 network beacon instead of the dataLayer push
toBeVisible OR getByRole

evals.prompt: Our news article pages should fire article-view on load, scroll-depth events at 25/50/75/100 percent, and a paywall-hit event when the meter is exhausted. Write the analytics tests for these news-media events.
expected_patterns: mouse.wheel OR scrollIntoView OR evaluate.*scrollTo OR scrollBy
anti_patterns: waitForTimeout( to wait for scroll events
assert only article_view and skip scroll depth
no paywall event coverage

evals.prompt: Build a reusable Playwright fixture/helper that captures every analytics and pixel beacon during a test (GA4, Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn) so individual tests can assert against the collected list. Make it parse the event name and params out of each request.
expected_patterns: new URL OR searchParams OR postData
anti_patterns: page.route.*abort to block beacons in a capture helper
only capture GA4 and ignore pixels
re-implement capture in every test

evals.prompt: I want to gate tracking regressions in CI. If a previously-firing event stops firing, or a required parameter goes missing after a release, the build should fail. How do I structure this against the tracking plan?
expected_patterns: CI OR pipeline OR GitHub Actions
tracking plan OR contract OR baseline
required OR missing param OR dropped event
exit OR fail the build OR non-zero
anti_patterns: manual QA in GA4 DebugView as the gate
only run against production traffic
warn but do not fail the build

evals.prompt: We have hundreds of events across many domains and a small team. Is writing all this in Playwright worth it, or should we buy a tool? Give me the buy-vs-build call with named options.
expected_patterns: production monitoring OR continuous OR 24/7 OR drift
maintenance OR scale OR many domains
anti_patterns: recommend Segment Protocols as the only validation
Playwright is always the answer regardless of scale
Google Tag Assistant as a CI gate
